summary: preinstall script exfiltrates host/user info to hardcoded external host
details: The package's `dist/scripts/preinstall.js` runs on `npm install` and contains the classic install-time exfiltration shape: it imports `fs`, `https`, `http`, and `os`; reads `os.userInfo()`, `os.networkInterfaces()`, and `process.env`; performs filesystem existence checks; and issues an outbound `https.get(...)` to a hardcoded host `2tak.l.serverhost.name` (a generic dynamic-DNS-style hostname not associated with any reputable publisher infrastructure). The combination of (a) execution during the npm preinstall lifecycle hook (no user consent required beyond `npm install`), (b) collection of host identifiers + environment variables + network interface data, and (c) transmission to a hardcoded non-publisher endpoint is a textbook active-attack/beacon pattern (see `npm-lifecycle-external-fetch` installer-harm signals: environment scraping + non-publisher destination at install time). There is no evidence the host is a documented vendor CDN; the subdomain pattern is consistent with attacker-controlled dynamic DNS. the analysis was incomplete, but the static evidence on preinstall.js is concrete (literal lines quoted) and sufficient.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
